8 In his days the Edomites revolted from under the dominion of Judah, and made themselves'a king.

Edomite, of the blood royal, who had been carried into Egypt from his childhood, returned into his own country, and raised some disturbances, but was not able to recover his throne, his subjects being over-awed by the garrisons which David had placed among them; but in the reign of Jehoram, the son of Jehoshaphat king of Judah, the Edomites revolted from under the dominion of Judah, and made themselves a king. Jehoram made some attempts to subdue them again, but could not prevail; so the…

into bed Notice the JST change to "hell." The Eastern text reads, "Behold I will cast her into a sickbed, and those who commit adultery with her into great tribulation, unless they repent of their deeds." Sickbed is an Aramaic idiom which means "an incurable disease."
18 And after all this the Lord smote him in his bowels with an incurable | disease, ;

into bed Notice the JST change to "hell." The Eastern text reads, "Behold I will cast her into a sickbed, and those who commit adultery with her into great tribulation, unless they repent of their deeds." Sickbed is an Aramaic idiom which means "an incurable disease."

19 And it came to pass, that in process of time, after the end of two years, his bowels fell cut by reason of his sickness; so he died of sore diseases. And his people made no burning for him, like the burning af his fathers. :
